name: Quadratic Formula Calculator
description: Industrial-grade quadratic equation solver with root analysis, discriminant evaluation, and process capability insights. Compliant with ISO 9001:2015 and Six Sigma DMAIC methodology.

inputs.label: Coefficient a (quadratic term)
inputs.businessContext: Leading coefficient of the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0. Must be non-zero for a valid quadratic.

businessContext_i18n.en: Leading coefficient of the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0. Must be non-zero for a valid quadratic.

inputs.label: Coefficient b (linear term)
inputs.businessContext: Linear coefficient influencing the vertex position and root symmetry.

businessContext_i18n.en: Linear coefficient influencing the vertex position and root symmetry.

inputs.label: Coefficient c (constant term)
inputs.businessContext: Constant term representing the y-intercept of the quadratic function.

businessContext_i18n.en: Constant term representing the y-intercept of the quadratic function.

inputs.label: Precision Mode
inputs.businessContext: Selects rounding and display precision: standard (4 decimals), high (8 decimals), engineering (scientific notation).

businessContext_i18n.en: Selects rounding and display precision: standard (4 decimals), high (8 decimals), engineering (scientific notation).

inputs.label: Allow Complex Roots
inputs.businessContext: When true, the calculator returns complex roots for negative discriminants. When false, it returns an error for non-real roots.

businessContext_i18n.en: When true, the calculator returns complex roots for negative discriminants. When false, it returns an error for non-real roots.

rules.message: Coefficient 'a' must be non-zero for a quadratic equation.

rules.condition: coefficient_a !== 0 && (coefficient_b * coefficient_b - 4 * coefficient_a * coefficient_c) < 0 && use_complex_roots === false
rules.message: Discriminant is negative. Enable 'Allow Complex Roots' or check input values.
Input magnitude may cause numerical instability. Consider scaling down.

thresholds.message: Discriminant is near zero. Roots may be repeated (double root).
Large coefficient ratio detected. Potential loss of significance in root calculation.

formulas.name: Discriminant (Δ)
formulas.description: Determines the nature of roots: Δ > 0 (two distinct real roots), Δ = 0 (one real double root), Δ < 0 (complex roots).

formulas.name: Square Root of Discriminant
formulas.description: Square root of the discriminant, used in the quadratic formula. For negative Δ, returns imaginary component.

formulas.name: First Root (x₁)
formulas.description: First solution of the quadratic equation using the positive square root.

formulas.name: Second Root (x₂)
formulas.description: Second solution of the quadratic equation using the negative square root.

formulas.name: Vertex x-coordinate
formulas.description: x-coordinate of the parabola vertex, representing the axis of symmetry.

formulas.name: Vertex y-coordinate
formulas.description: y-coordinate of the parabola vertex, representing the minimum or maximum value.
formulas.expression: coefficient_a * vertex_x * vertex_x + coefficient_b * vertex_x + coefficient_c

formulas.name: Sum of Roots (Vieta)
formulas.description: Sum of the two roots, equal to -b/a per Vieta's formulas.

primary.label: Primary Roots
primary.description: The main calculated roots of the quadratic equation. If complex, displayed as a+bi.

breakdown.label: Detailed Breakdown
breakdown.description: Component values of the quadratic formula calculation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Loss of Significance Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: When coefficients differ by orders of magnitude, floating-point precision may cause inaccurate roots.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Use high precision mode or scale inputs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Near-Zero Discriminant
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Discriminant close to zero may indicate a double root but numerical rounding can cause small imaginary parts.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Check discriminant threshold and consider symbolic simplification.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Large |a| Coefficient
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Large absolute value of 'a' can amplify rounding errors in root calculation.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Normalize equation by dividing by a if possible.

suggestedActions.label: Scale Inputs
suggestedActions.description: If coefficients are very large or small, divide the entire equation by a common factor to improve numerical stability.

suggestedActions.label: Verify Roots by Substitution
suggestedActions.description: Substitute calculated roots back into the original equation to verify correctness within tolerance.

suggestedActions.label: Switch to High Precision Mode
suggestedActions.description: Enable high precision mode to reduce rounding errors in critical applications.

suggestedActions.label: Enable Complex Roots for Negative Discriminant
suggestedActions.description: If the discriminant is negative, enable complex roots to obtain full solution set.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Adjusted confidence level based on input quality and numerical stability (0 to 1).
